title: Chrononauts & Time Travel
summary: Time travelers and timeline-viewing technology — from Andy Basiago to Project Looking Glass.
markdown:
**Chrononauts** — time travelers — are a recurring subject in the archive, tied to
Unimetrix 1's own claim to communicate from the far future by moving along its timeline.

Two figures anchor the theme. **Andy Basiago**, who appears as an interview guest, is
described in the sessions in connection with **Project Pegasus** and government time-travel
and teleportation programs. And **Project Looking Glass** is discussed as a technology for
**viewing different timelines** — Unimetrix 1 describes it being used, especially around
2012–2014, to organize data across parallel realities and forecast outcomes.

The archive's other great chrononaut is **Ori**, described in the earlier material as a
stranded time-traveler. Together these threads reflect one of Unimetrix 1's core premises:
that time "does not exist" as a fixed line, that parallel and temporal dimensions run at
different rates, and that the present timeline is being actively **course-corrected** by
intelligences reaching back from the future — the same premise behind the
[Interstellar Alliance's](/lore/interstellar-alliance/) interventions.
